matches.snippet: Dear Dirty Dumpling, foostherfather of fingalls and dotthergills.
matches.reasoning: Fingal is a variant/epithet name for Finn MacCool in Gaelic tradition; Joyce's 'fingalls' echoes this directly

matches.snippet: Every evening at lighting up o'clock sharp and until further notice in Feenichts Playhouse.
matches.reasoning: Feenichts is a garbled spelling of Fenians/Fionn; invokes both the mythic Fianna warriors and the Finn/Fionn name

matches.matched_text: fern may cald us until firn make cold
matches.snippet: While fern may cald us until firn make cold. The Mime of Micky Nick and the Maggies
matches.reasoning: Cryptic doubling of Finn-like elements: 'fern' and 'firn' echo 'Finn'; the phrasing suggests cyclical time/fall-and-resurrection

matches.snippet: Jests, jokes, jigs and jorums for the Wake lent from the properties of the late cemented Mr T. M. Finnegan R.I.C.
matches.reasoning: Finnegan is the hod-carrier/bricklayer hero explicitly named; 'T. M.' initials; R.I.C. (Royal Irish Constabulary) suggests Irish context and law/authority figure merging with Finn
